name: Chocolate Chunk Brownies
recipeIngredient: 9 tbsp Unsalted butter, cut into tablespoons
1 1/2 cup Coarsely chopped walnut halves, divided
9 oz Swiss dark chocolate, cut into 1-inch chunks -- divided
recipeInstructions:
1. Position a rack in the center of the oven and preheat to 325 degrees F.
2. Line a 13-by-9 1/2-inch baking pan with double thickness of aluminum foil extending 2 inches beyond the two short ends. Fold the overhang down along the sides of the pan.
3. Butter the bottom of the foil-lined pan.
4. In a medium bowl, stir together the flour, baking soda, baking powder, and salt.
5. Place the semisweet chocolate in a large bowl.
6. In a medium saucepan, combine the sugar, butter, corn syrup, and water. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon until the butter melts, the sugar is dissolved, and the mixture comes to a boil.
7. Remove the pan from the heat and pour the hot syrup over the chocolate. Let the mixture stand for 1 to 2 minutes to melt the chocolate.
8. Whisk until smooth.
9. One at a time, whisk in the eggs, blending until smooth. Whisk in the vanilla and the flour mixture, mixing until the batter is smooth.
10. Using a rubber spatula, fold in 1 cup of the walnuts and 6 ounces of the Swiss chocolate chunks.
11. Scrape the batter into the prepared pan and spread it evenly with a spatula.
12. Sprinkle the remaining 1/2 cup of walnuts and 3 ounces of chocolate chunks over the top of the batter.
13. Bake the brownies for 40 to 50 minutes, or until a cake tester or toothpick inserted into the center comes out with a few moist crumbs clinging to it.
14. Cool the brownies in the pan set on a wire rack. When completely cool, cover the pan with plastic wrap and let the brownies set at room temperature for at least 6 hours or overnight.
15. Invert the brownies onto a large plate or cutting board. Remove the pan and carefully peel off the foil. Invert again onto a smooth cutting surface and cut into 24 bars.
16. Store the brownies at room temperature in an airtight container for up to five days.
